methodology_note: Constrained scoring rule. Each asset starts with a thesis conviction and macro support score derived from the five live PolyBridge probabilities. Final USD notionals use square-root scaling, asset caps, a $50,000 gross notional budget, and rounding to the nearest $500.

positions.thesis: Fixed supply, maximum decentralisation, actual PMF.
macro_evidence: Crypto reform is priced at 55%, which is the main macro tailwind for BTC.
Fed cuts are only priced at 11%, so this BTC long leans more on the policy backdrop than on a near-term liquidity easing impulse.
positions.rationale: Sized at $11,500 from a 85% fixed thesis conviction, a 48.3% macro support score, and a $18,000 asset cap.

positions.thesis: AI tailwind remains structural, but macro downside still matters.
macro_evidence: Recession is only priced at 16%, which keeps the SPX long viable even though the market does not expect early Fed easing.
Hormuz reopening at 18% and WTI > $90 at 50% shape the energy shock risk around SPX.
positions.rationale: Sized at $6,500 from a 68% fixed thesis conviction, a 41.5% macro support score, and a $12,000 asset cap.

positions.thesis: Declining sequencer revenue, ongoing token unlocks, fading narrative.
macro_evidence: Crypto reform is priced at 55%; higher reform odds weaken the short, lower reform odds strengthen it.
Fed cuts are only priced at 11%, which limits any easy-money tailwind for alt beta and keeps the OP short thesis intact.
positions.rationale: Sized at $6,000 from a 72% fixed thesis conviction, a 47.3% macro support score, and a $10,000 asset cap.

positions.thesis: High token supply, no clear PMF, founder conviction signals low.
macro_evidence: BERA short conviction depends heavily on crypto reform at 55% because a strong policy tailwind can squeeze weaker alts.
Low Fed cut odds at 11% mean this short is not fighting a strong easing narrative, even though recession risk itself is only 16%.
positions.rationale: Sized at $4,500 from a 69% fixed thesis conviction, a 45.1% macro support score, and a $8,000 asset cap.

positions.thesis: Geopolitics are highly uncertain, so conviction stays conditional.
macro_evidence: WTI > $90 is priced at 50% while Hormuz reopening is priced at 18%.
Bull support is 64.4% and bear support is 35.6%, so the oil signal is handled conservatively.
positions.rationale: Sized at $1,000 from a 50% fixed thesis conviction, a 64.4% macro support score, and a $2,000 asset cap.
